capabilities.quotaProtection: Uses cached/replay provider evidence and starts/stops runtime loops for proof.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Does not prove fresh provider odds unless the live-provider variant is used.

capabilities.quotaProtection: Requires THE_ODDS_API_KEY from environment or ignored local secret file; uses quota caps and one-event scope.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Operator-triggered only; not an installed unattended provider polling service.

capabilities.quotaProtection: Cached mode spends no provider quota. Provider proof is opt-in and capped by MaxProviderProofRuns.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Continuous only while the local command/process is running; installed OS service remains missing.

capabilities.quotaProtection: Replay/local result mode is quota-free; live result ingestion is opt-in and capped.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Production official-result auto-settlement is not fully owned as an installed service.

capabilities.quotaProtection: Uses stored provider reference snapshots; does not call The Odds API.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Standalone command is one-shot; repeated reseeding requires the supervisor loop.

capabilities.quotaProtection: Lifecycle scheduler does not call the odds provider.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Scheduler can be repeated by the supervisor, but installed unattended ownership remains P1.

capabilities.area: local OS persistence for internal tester runtime
capabilities.quotaProtection: Default scheduled-task plan starts cached backend/Expo/supervisor/result-poller flow without provider proof; live provider modes require explicit flags and THE_ODDS_API_KEY.
capabilities.currentLimitation: Local Windows Scheduled Task path is operator-triggered and machine/user specific; it is not production service monitoring.

capabilities.area: user-logon fallback for internal tester runtime
capabilities.quotaProtection: Default Startup launcher starts cached backend/Expo/supervisor/result-poller flow without provider proof; live provider modes require explicit flags and THE_ODDS_API_KEY.
capabilities.currentLimitation: User Startup launcher runs only when the Windows user logs in; it is a local fallback, not a scheduled daemon or production service.

conclusion: Local runtime has quota-free cached onboarding, operator-triggered live-provider onboarding, continuous-while-command-runs supervisor/result-poller loops, one-shot maker/lifecycle commands, and two operator-triggered local persistence options. No production service ownership exists yet.
